title: Polish modular document viewer typography and source scrolling
description:
Address follow-up usability issues in the modular document-rendering slice.

## Background

The first delivery added dedicated renderers for CSV and text/code content, but there are still a few presentation issues in the rich viewers:
- CSV/text/code content uses a smaller default font than the original markdown viewer.
- Rich source-code rendering creates a separate horizontally scrollable block per line, which looks fragmented and makes wide lines awkward to inspect.
- Rich preview caps still default to 200 items instead of the requested 1000-item default.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] CSV and text/code rich viewers use the same default content font sizing as the original markdown viewer while preserving their existing formatting affordances.
- [ ] Rich source-code rendering uses a single horizontally scrollable code block rather than per-line scroll regions, while preserving line numbers and line-level search highlighting.
- [ ] Default rich preview caps increase from 200 to 1000 items for text-like previews, with renderer-specific notices remaining accurate.
- [ ] Existing web-side renderer tests cover the new behavior and the configured web CI gate passes cleanly.

## Notes

This is a focused follow-up to the completed modular document rendering story.
